---
title: "Infographic: Toxic chemcial exposure in our menstrual products"
author: "Bella Kormos"
date: "18 March, 2026"
format:
  html:
    mainfont: "EB Garamond"
    number-sections: false # op-eds don't usually number sections
    theme: cosmo # clean professional theme
    fontsize: 1.1em  # slightly larger for readability
    linestretch: 1.6 # comfortable line spacing
    smooth-scroll: true # smooth scrolling between sections
editor: visual
---

## The Chemicals and Human Health Risks from Menstrual Products 
![](menstrual-infographic.jpg){width=150% fig-pos="H"}

**Artist Statement:**
The audience in mind while creating this infographic was  all menstruators, but specifically college students who may not even consider reading the ingredient lists in menstrual products. Students have so much on their plate balancing classes with life, and usually don't pay attention or pay more money for organic, non-toxic personal care products including menstrual products. I hope this infographic can bring awareness to the toxic effects of many common menstrual brands, and educate menstruators about possible chemical exposures. I did my best to put lots of scientific evidence into a readable and accessible format. I used the scientific findings from my ENVS 104 Final Report, images, and drew up this infographic on my ipad. I also used my coding skills in R-studio to convert this infographic into a website to be more easily accessible to anyone with the link. This work promotes understanding of clean menstrual brands and encourages menstruators to take toxic chemicals seriously. 

**Works Cited:**

Fallon, M. (2024) “Reckless Regulation: The Frightening Truth Behind Feminine Hygiene Products”, Elon Law Review. https://eloncdn.blob.core.windows.net/eu3/sites/996/2024/02/Fallon.pdf

Regional Health-Americas TL, "Menstrual Health: A Neglected Public Health Problem," Lancet Regional Health – Americas 15 (2022): 100399, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.lana.2022.100399.

Sosa-Ferrera, Zoraida, Cristina Mahugo-Santana, and José Juan Santana-Rodríguez. "Analytical Methodologies for the Determination of Endocrine Disrupting Compounds in Biological and Environmental Samples." BioMed Research International 2013 (2013): 674838. https://doi.org/10.1155/2013/674838.
